codeReview.summary: The commit only adds auth lifecycle tests, and the added coverage matches the resolved-source persistence design for autodetected kiro-cli and kiro-ide stores. I found no high-confidence actionable defects in the changed code or the exercised auth paths.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The auth-source-worker skill is ambiguous about how to report the 'failing tests first' exception path for assurance-only features. This worker documented that the behavior already existed and the new tests passed immediately, but still marked followedProcedure=true, which makes it hard to distinguish full compliance from a justified exception.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /var/home/milton/Workspaces/M/giro/.factory/skills/auth-source-worker/SKILL.md:21-25 requires failing tests first but says to explain why when that is not possible; /home/milton/.factory/missions/0d379347-5b34-4fb5-830f-2b6650044b7f/handoffs/2026-03-29T20-49-18-764Z__autodetected-source-refresh-persistence__0d65b6a5-49c0-476d-825a-27a107d4854c.json:69-72 records followedProcedure=true alongside an explanation that the tests passed immediately because the implementation already handled the behavior.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the transcript skeleton and commit 7c25d87f9b944f3460cc5156b93edef24e405820 and found no blocking or non-blocking code defects; the only follow-up is a skill-reporting ambiguity for test-first exceptions.
